meaning: adj.没有人住的,无人占领的,空闭的,未占领的
examples: I sat down at the nearest unoccupied table.
The house was unoccupied at the time of the explosion.
The house was unoccupied and in a bad state of disrepair.
Formally opened in January 2008, the tower remains unoccupied because of fears about its soundness.
It seemed strange that a boat like this, a sturdy, viable boat, would be unoccupied.
